1→# Requirements: Rune
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v5.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for first working implementation. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Adapter Registry
    11→
    12→- [x] **AREG-01**: User can store a new adapter record with metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source) via AdapterRegistry.store()
    13→- [x] **AREG-02**: User can retrieve an adapter record by ID via AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id()
    14→- [x] **AREG-03**: User can query adapters by task_type via AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type()
    15→- [x] **AREG-04**: User can list all non-archived adapters via AdapterRegistry.list_all()
    16→- [x] **AREG-05**: AdapterRegistry uses SQLite with WAL mode and engine-parameterized constructor for shared database access
    17→
    18→### Inference
    19→
    20→- [x] **INF-01**: Abstract InferenceProvider interface with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), list_adapters() methods
    21→- [x] **INF-02**: VLLMProvider implementation with full LoRA hot-loading support (POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, /v1/unload_lora_adapter)
    22→- [x] **INF-03**: OllamaProvider implementation for inference via Ollama API (LoRA support where Ollama supports it)
    23→- [x] **INF-04**: Provider factory/registry for selecting backend by configuration (env var or config file)
    24→- [x] **INF-05**: User can generate with a specific loaded adapter by passing adapter name as model parameter
    25→- [x] **INF-06**: User can load multiple adapters simultaneously for composition (provider-dependent, graceful degradation)
    26→- [x] **INF-07**: Per-step model/provider configuration — agent can use different models or providers for different steps (e.g., one model for generation, another for reflection)
    27→
    28→### Agent Loop
    29→
    30→- [x] **AGENT-01**: generate_node calls InferenceProvider.generate() with task description and optional adapter, returning generated code (backend-agnostic)
    31→- [x] **AGENT-02**: execute_node runs generated code in a sandboxed subprocess with timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exit_code/tests_passed
    32→- [x] **AGENT-03**: reflect_node accumulates trajectory data (attempt count, code, results) without LLM call
    33→- [x] **AGENT-04**: save_trajectory_node persists trajectory via record_trajectory() and sets outcome
    34→- [x] **AGENT-05**: RuneState includes session_id field for trajectory persistence
    35→- [x] **AGENT-06**: Agent loop closes end-to-end: generate → execute → reflect → retry/save with should_retry routing
    36→
    37→### Training Pipeline
    38→
    39→- [x] **TRAIN-01**: User can record a coding trajectory as structured JSON via record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)
    40→- [x] **TRAIN-02**: User can convert trajectory data to SFT chat format via format_for_sft()
    41→- [x] **TRAIN-03**: User can create a QLoRA config with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype via build_qlora_config()
    42→- [x] **TRAIN-04**: User can apply a LoRA adapter to a base model via apply_lora_adapter()
    43→- [x] **TRAIN-05**: QLoRA training pipeline runs end-to-end: trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → store in registry
    44→- [x] **TRAIN-06**: training-svc exposes POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking
    45→- [x] **TRAIN-07**: training-svc pyproject.toml declares model-training as workspace dependency
    46→
    47→### Doc-to-LoRA Hypernetwork
    48→
    49→- [x] **DTOL-01**: DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture
    50→- [x] **DTOL-02**: Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s)
    51→- [x] **DTOL-03**: Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format)
    52→- [x] **DTOL-04**: training-svc exposes POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint for Doc-to-LoRA training
    53→
    54→### Evaluation & Kill-Switch
    55→
    56→- [x] **EVAL-01**: User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset()
    57→- [x] **EVAL-02**: User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k()
    58→- [x] **EVAL-03**: Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)
    59→
    60→### Infrastructure
    61→
    62→- [x] **INFRA-01**: lora-server Dockerfile uses vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image (not python:3.12-slim)
    63→- [x] **INFRA-02**: lora-server sets VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True environment variable
    64→- [x] **INFRA-03**: docker-compose resolves port conflict (api-service and lora-server on different host ports)
    65→- [x] **INFRA-04**: model-training pyproject.toml adds GPU dependencies (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) with TYPE_CHECKING guards
    66→- [x] **INFRA-05**: All GPU imports deferred inside function bodies (not top-level) for CPU-only CI compatibility
    67→
    68→## Future Requirements
    69→
    70→Deferred to v6+. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    71→
    72→### Evolution
    73→
    74→- **EVOL-01**: Evolution operator scores adapter fitness based on HumanEval pass rate
    75→- **EVOL-02**: Evolution operator performs adapter crossover via weight interpolation
    76→- **EVOL-03**: Evolution operator archives low-fitness adapters
    77→
    78→### Advanced Features
    79→
    80→- **ADV-01**: Embedding-based automatic adapter selection via vector similarity search
    81→- **ADV-02**: HITL UI with session monitoring, adapter library browser, trajectory viewer
    82→- **ADV-03**: Docker container isolation for code execution (replace subprocess sandbox)
    83→
    84→## Out of Scope
    85→
    86→Explicitly excluded. Documented to prevent scope creep.
    87→
    88→| Feature | Reason |
    89→|---------|--------|
    90→| Hardware validation phase | User deferred — will validate hardware separately |
    91→| Tensor parallelism (TP=2) | vLLM bug #21471 — corrupted output on PCIe GPUs without NVLink |
    92→| Adapter merging into base weights | Loses hot-swap composability; vLLM Punica kernels handle LoRA with near-zero overhead |
    93→| Cloud API inference | Hard constraint: local-first, no cloud dependencies for inference. Provider abstraction supports local backends (vLLM, Ollama) only |
    94→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM — must schedule sequentially |
    95→| Streaming responses in agent loop | Agent needs complete code, not token stream; add to API layer separately if needed |
    96→| Multi-tenant isolation | Single-user local system; session_id provides sufficient organization |
    97→| evolution-svc implementation | Depends on populated adapter library from multiple sessions; v6+ |
    98→
    99→## Traceability
   100→
   101→Which phases cover which requirements. Updated during roadmap creation.
   102→

   104→|-------------|-------|--------|

    64→## Accumulated Context
    65→
    66→### Decisions
    67→
    68→Decisions are logged in PROJECT.md Key Decisions table.
    69→Recent decisions affecting v5.0:
    70→
    71→- vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image used; openai removed from pip install (bundled in vLLM image); VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True enables runtime adapter loading
    72→- lora-server host port changed from 8000 to 8100 in docker-compose; container-internal port stays 8000 (startup.sh unchanged)
    73→- rune_data:/data shared volume added to api and lora-server in docker-compose for cross-service SQLite access
    74→- Hardware validation deferred — user will validate hardware separately; not a roadmap phase
    75→- Provider abstraction added — InferenceProvider interface (INF-01) with VLLMProvider (INF-02), OllamaProvider (INF-03), factory (INF-04), per-step model/provider config (INF-07)
    76→- AGENT-01 is backend-agnostic — calls InferenceProvider.generate(), not VLLMClient directly
    77→- Build order: adapter-registry → inference abstraction + providers → agent loop + trajectory → QLoRA training → kill-switch gate
    78→- INFRA-01/02/03 in Phase 19 (lora-server config + port fix); INFRA-04/05 in Phase 21 (GPU deps)
    79→- evolution-svc explicitly out of scope for v5.0 (deferred to v6+)
    80→- bfloat16 hardcoded in build_qlora_config (float16 causes silent NaN loss at 7B scale)
    81→- max_loras=2, max_lora_rank=64, gpu_memory_utilization=0.80 to avoid VRAM OOM at startup
    82→- Engine from sqlalchemy.engine not sqlmodel — SQLModel does not re-export Engine (discovered 18-01, confirmed 18-02)
    83→- Session-per-method pattern with expire_on_commit=False + expunge() — required to prevent DetachedInstanceError when returning records from closed sessions (established 18-01)
    84→- WAL test must use file-based engine (tmp_path), not :memory: — WAL has no practical effect on in-memory SQLite
    85→- memory_engine fixture: create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:") with function scope gives per-test isolation at zero cleanup cost
    86→- [Phase 19-01]: asyncio_mode=auto added to inference lib pyproject.toml — lib-scoped pytest overrides root config
    87→- [Phase 19-01]: VLLMProvider.generate() passes adapter_id as model param — vLLM LoRA routing mechanism requires lora_name in the model field
    88→- [Phase 19-01]: VLLM_BASE_URL default set to port 8100 (api-service owns port 8000)
    89→- [Phase 19-03]: os.environ.get() used in factory.py instead of os.getenv() — mypy cannot narrow os.getenv(key, str_default) return type to str; explicit variable annotations resolve the issue
    90→- [Phase 19-03]: Env var reads placed inside get_provider() function body (not module level) — allows monkeypatch.setenv() to work in tests; module-level reads captured at import time
    91→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR read inside function body for monkeypatch testability (same pattern as Phase 19 factory.py)
    92→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: record_trajectory() extended with keyword-only task_description/task_type/adapter_ids args for save_trajectory_node compatibility
    93→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: format_for_sft() uses reversed(steps) to find last tests_passed=True step as assistant content
    94→- [Phase 20-02]: RUNE_MODEL and RUNE_EXEC_TIMEOUT env vars read inside function bodies for monkeypatch testability — same pattern as Phase 19 factory.py
    95→- [Phase 20-02]: py.typed markers added to inference and model-training libs — correct PEP 561 solution for mypy strict import-untyped compliance
    96→- [Phase 20-02]: reflect_node uses list concatenation (state["trajectory"] + [step]) not .append() — LangGraph requires immutable state updates
    97→- [Phase 21-01]: bfloat16 compute dtype set in BitsAndBytesConfig, NOT LoraConfig — LoraConfig is purely about LoRA layer structure
    98→- [Phase 21-01]: No modules_to_save in LoraConfig — would break vLLM adapter loading (include embed_tokens/lm_head in saved PEFT artifact)
    99→- [Phase 21-01]: sys.modules injection pattern for mocking deferred GPU imports in CPU CI — unittest.mock.patch("peft.X") fails when peft is not installed
   100→- [Phase 21-01]: RUNE_ADAPTER_DIR/RUNE_BASE_MODEL/RUNE_DATABASE_URL env vars read inside function bodies for monkeypatch testability
   101→- [Phase 21-02]: Deferred import of model_training.trainer inside _run_training_job body — prevents GPU lib import at startup in CPU-only environments (same INFRA-05 pattern)
   102→- [Phase 21-02]: JOB_STORE as module-level dict — state is lost on restart, acceptable for single-user local MVP
   103→- [Phase 21-02]: _run_training_job is a regular (non-async) function — FastAPI BackgroundTasks runs it in thread pool executor
   104→- [Phase 21-02]: Mock pattern: patch training_svc.routers.training._run_training_job in tests to prevent model_training.trainer GPU import
   105→- [Phase 22-01]: DocToLoraHypernetwork uses _LazyHypernetworkProxy — real nn.Module subclass built on first instantiation to keep module importable without torch (INFRA-05)
   106→- [Phase 22-01]: save_hypernetwork_adapter: modules_to_save=None — vLLM rejects embed_tokens/lm_head in adapter artifacts (consistent with Phase 21-01 decision)
   107→- [Phase 22-01]: Torch tests use hidden_dim=32, num_layers=1-2 to keep CPU CI fast — large linear weight_head (8192 x 3.67M) hangs indefinitely on CPU with default 7B params
   108→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: run_humaneval_subset accepts Optional completions dict — None raises NotImplementedError; inference wiring deferred to orchestration level
   109→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: data/ directory un-ignored via local .gitignore in evaluation/src/evaluation/ — root .gitignore has global data/ exclusion
   110→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: sys.executable used in subprocess (not hardcoded python) — matches current venv Python in all execution environments
   111→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: _run_hypernetwork_job uses first trajectory_id from request.trajectory_ids — single trajectory drives the hypernetwork forward pass
   112→- [Phase 23-01]: Use training_svc.storage.engine (not RUNE_DATABASE_URL) in _run_hypernetwork_job — service-level function shares service DB, ensures QLoRA and hypernetwork adapters in same database
   113→- [Phase 23-01]: model_training added to workspace-packages mypy override block (not GPU libs block) — it is a local workspace package, not a GPU-only library
   114→- [Phase 23-01]: All new imports deferred inside _run_hypernetwork_job function body per INFRA-05 — allows patch("training_svc.storage.engine", ...) in tests

    30→
    31→# Phase 22 Plan 01: DocToLoraHypernetwork Summary
    32→
    33→**One-liner:** Perceiver-based hypernetwork generating rank-8 LoRA adapters from token IDs in a single forward pass via lazy nn.Module proxy pattern for CPU-only importability.
    34→
    35→## What Was Built
    36→
    37→`DocToLoraHypernetwork` in `libs/model-training/src/model_training/hypernetwork.py` — a Perceiver architecture that cross-attends over token embeddings with learned latents to produce PEFT-compatible LoRA weight matrices for `q_proj` and `v_proj` across all transformer layers.
    38→
    39→Key components:
    40→- **Lazy proxy pattern**: `DocToLoraHypernetwork` is a `_LazyHypernetworkProxy` at module level; the real `nn.Module` subclass is built and cached on first instantiation. This keeps the module importable without `torch` installed (INFRA-05 compliance).
    41→- **Perceiver architecture**: learned token embedding (not the 7B base model's), 32 latent vectors (256 dim), cross-attention (latents attend over token embeddings), self-attention stack (depth=4, heads=8), linear weight head projecting to all LoRA parameters.
    42→- **forward() output**: PEFT state_dict keys following `base_model.model.model.layers.{i}.self_attn.{module}.lora_{A|B}.weight` with lora_A shape (rank, hidden_dim) and lora_B shape (hidden_dim, rank). Returns first batch element.
    43→- **save_hypernetwork_adapter()**: writes `adapter_model.safetensors` via deferred `safetensors.torch.save_file` import and `adapter_config.json` with correct PEFT fields. `modules_to_save=None` prevents vLLM rejection.
    44→
    45→## Tasks Completed
    46→
    47→| Task | Name | Commit | Files |
    48→|------|------|--------|-------|

    50→| GREEN | Implementation + __init__.py update | 4f91ad3 | hypernetwork.py (268 lines), __init__.py, test fixes |

    66→- **Fix:** Tests that exercise forward/construction use `hidden_dim=32, num_layers=1-2` to stay fast on CPU. Latents shape test keeps `num_latents=32, latent_dim=256` (the defaults being tested) but uses small `hidden_dim`.
    67→- **Files modified:** `libs/model-training/tests/test_hypernetwork.py`

    56→# Phase 22 Plan 02: Evaluation Metrics Implementation Summary
    57→
    58→**calculate_pass_at_k unbiased estimator (Chen 2021), run_kill_switch_gate 5%-threshold verdict, and run_humaneval_subset subprocess executor with bundled 20-task HumanEval JSON — 21 tests green**
    59→
    60→## Performance
    61→
    62→- **Duration:** 12 min
    63→- **Started:** 2026-03-05T21:50:34Z
    64→- **Completed:** 2026-03-05T22:02:52Z
    65→- **Tasks:** 2 (both TDD: 4 commits — 2 RED + 2 GREEN)
    66→- **Files modified:** 5
    67→
    68→## Accomplishments
    69→
    70→- `calculate_pass_at_k`: implements unbiased estimator `1 - prod((n-c-i)/(n-i))` with ValueError guard for n_correct > n_samples; handles edge cases (all correct, n-c < k)
    71→- `run_kill_switch_gate`: computes relative delta with 1e-9 zero-baseline guard, returns PASS when `adapter_pass1 >= baseline_pass1 * 1.05`, prints summary
    72→- `run_humaneval_subset`: loads 20-task bundled JSON, runs each task via `subprocess.run(sys.executable)`, returns structured pass/fail results dict
    73→- `humaneval_subset.json`: HumanEval/0-19 bundled statically (has_close_elements through sort_numbers) — no network dependency

[project]
name = "model-training"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Training pipelines"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "shared",
    "torch>=2.0.0",
    "peft>=0.18.0",
    "bitsandbytes>=0.45.0",
    "transformers>=4.47.0",
    "trl>=0.16.0",
    "datasets>=3.0.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/model_training"]

[tool.mypy]
strict = true
exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs"]
plugins = []
mypy_path = ["../shared/src"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
pythonpath = ["src", "../shared/src"]
testpaths = ["tests"]
addopts = "--cov=model_training --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing"

[tool.uv.sources]
shared = { workspace = true }

input.description: Read top of hypernetwork.py to understand imports

content.content:
"""DocToLoraHypernetwork: Perceiver-based instant LoRA adapter generation.

Generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from token IDs in a single forward pass.
Distinct from the QLoRA gradient-descent path (Phase 21) — this produces adapters
in <1s by cross-attending over token embeddings with learned latents.

IMPORTANT: All GPU imports (torch, safetensors) are deferred inside function/method
bodies per INFRA-05 pattern — this module is importable in CPU-only CI.

Usage:
    from model_training.hypernetwork import (
        DocToLoraHypernetwork,
        save_hypernetwork_adapter,
    )

    model = DocToLoraHypernetwork(input_dim=32000)
    weights = model(token_ids)
    save_hypernetwork_adapter(weights, "/tmp/adapter", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B")
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Sequence

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    import torch


def _build_hypernetwork_class() -> type:
    """Build and return DocToLoraHypernetwork as a real nn.Module subclass.

    This function is called the first time DocToLoraHypernetwork is instantiated
    so that torch.nn.Module is only imported when needed (INFRA-05 pattern).
    """
    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    import torch.nn as nn  # noqa: PLC0415

    class _DocToLoraHypernetwork(nn.Module):
        """Perceiver-based hypernetwork that generates LoRA adapter weights.

        Given a sequence of token IDs (from a document/trajectory), produces a
        PEFT-compatible state_dict with lora_A and lora_B matrices for every
        target_module across all transformer layers — in a single forward pass.

        Architecture:
        - Lightweight learned token embedding (not the 7B base model's embedding)
        - Learnable latent array (num_latents x latent_dim)
        - Cross-attention: latents attend over token embeddings
No skills directory

    55→### v5.0 First Implementation (In Progress)
    56→
    57→**Milestone Goal:** Transform wireframe stubs into a working end-to-end system — adapter registry to provider-agnostic inference to agent loop to QLoRA training to Doc-to-LoRA kill-switch gate — running entirely on local hardware.
    58→
    59→- [x] **Phase 18: Adapter Registry** — SQLite-backed CRUD hub that all other components read from and write to (2/2 plans complete)
    60→- [x] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration (3/3 plans complete)
    61→- [x] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence (2/2 plans complete)
    62→- [x] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch (completed 2026-03-05)
    63→- [x] **Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate** — Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork + evaluation lib measuring the 5% Pass@1 improvement threshold (completed 2026-03-06)
    64→- [x] **Phase 23: Integration Fix & Quality Gate** — Fix hypernetwork→registry gap (DTOL-04), xdist race, mypy strict errors, ruff violations (gap closure from audit) (completed 2026-03-06)
    65→- [ ] **Phase 24: Phase 22 Verification & Dependency Hygiene** — Create missing Phase 22 VERIFICATION.md, fix undeclared dependencies, fix SUMMARY frontmatter
    66→
    67→## Phase Details
    68→
    69→### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
    70→**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
    71→**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
    72→**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
    73→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    74→  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
    75→  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
    76→  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
    77→  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
    78→  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
    79→**Plans**: 2 plans
    80→
    81→Plans:
    82→- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
    83→- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05
    84→
    85→### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
    86→**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
    87→**Depends on**: Phase 18
    88→**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
    89→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    90→  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
    91→  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
    92→  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
    93→  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
    94→  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
    95→  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
    96→**Plans**: 3 plans
    97→
    98→Plans:
    99→- [x] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage — DONE 2026-03-05
   100→- [x] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix — DONE 2026-03-05
   101→- [x] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs — DONE 2026-03-05
   102→
   103→### Phase 20: Agent Loop
   104→**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
   105→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
   106→**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
   107→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   108→  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
   109→  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
   110→  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
   111→  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
   112→  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
   113→**Plans**: 2 plans
   114→
   115→Plans:
   116→- [x] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep — DONE 2026-03-05
   117→- [x] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites — DONE 2026-03-05
   118→
   119→### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
   120→**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
   121→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
   122→**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
   123→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   124→  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
   125→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   126→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   127→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
   128→  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
   129→**Plans**: 2 plans
   130→
   131→Plans:
   132→- [x] 21-01-PLAN.md — Implement model-training library: peft_utils, config, train_qlora orchestrator, GPU deps, mypy overrides — DONE 2026-03-05
   133→- [x] 21-02-PLAN.md — Wire training-svc endpoints: POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id} with background job dispatch — DONE 2026-03-05
   134→
   135→### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
   136→**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
   137→**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
   138→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
   139→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   140→  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
   141→  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
   142→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   143→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   144→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
   145→**Plans**: 3 plans
   146→
   147→Plans:
   148→- [x] 22-01-PLAN.md — DocToLoraHypernetwork Perceiver module with rank-8 LoRA weight generation and PEFT serialization — DONE 2026-03-05
   149→- [ ] 22-02-PLAN.md — Evaluation lib: calculate_pass_at_k, run_humaneval_subset with bundled 20-task data, run_kill_switch_gate verdict
   150→- [ ] 22-03-PLAN.md — Wire POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint with background task dispatch
   151→
   152→### Phase 23: Integration Fix & Quality Gate
   153→**Goal**: Close audit gaps — wire hypernetwork adapter registration and fix mypy strict errors (xdist race and ruff violations already resolved)
   154→**Depends on**: Phase 22
   155→**Requirements**: DTOL-04 (integration fix)
   156→**Gap Closure**: Closes gaps from v5.0-MILESTONE-AUDIT.md
   157→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   158→  1. `_run_hypernetwork_job` calls `AdapterRegistry.store()` after saving adapter to disk — hypernetwork adapters discoverable via `retrieve_by_id`/`list_all`
   159→  2. training-svc tests pass under xdist parallel execution (`uv run pytest -x` with default workers) with zero SQLite race conditions
   160→  3. `uv run mypy services/training-svc/src/` exits 0 with no attr-defined/operator errors on DocToLoraHypernetwork usage
   161→  4. `uv run ruff check libs/model-training/tests/ libs/evaluation/tests/` exits 0 with zero violations
   162→**Plans**: 1 plan
   163→
   164→Plans:
   165→- [x] 23-01-PLAN.md — Wire AdapterRegistry.store() into _run_hypernetwork_job, fix mypy model_training overrides, add integration test — DONE 2026-03-06
   166→
   167→### Phase 24: Phase 22 Verification & Dependency Hygiene
   168→**Goal**: Close 6 partial requirements by adding missing dependency declarations, fixing SUMMARY frontmatter, and formally verifying Phase 22's implementations
   169→**Depends on**: Phase 23
   170→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03 (verification gap from audit)
   171→**Gap Closure**: Closes gaps from v5.0-MILESTONE-AUDIT.md
   172→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   173→  1. `model-training/pyproject.toml` declares `adapter-registry` and `safetensors` as dependencies
   174→  2. 22-01-SUMMARY.md frontmatter uses `requirements-completed` field (not `requirements`)
   175→  3. Phase 22 has a VERIFICATION.md confirming DTOL-01/02/03 and EVAL-01/02/03 are satisfied
   176→  4. `uv run pytest -x` passes all tests with zero regressions

   178→

input.description: Read adapter-registry pyproject.toml to see how the package is named

content.content:
[project]
name = "adapter-registry"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "LoRA adapter metadata registry backed by SQLite via SQLModel"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "sqlmodel>=0.0.31",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-cov>=5.0.0",
    "ruff>=0.8.0",
    "mypy>=1.0.0",
]

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["src/adapter_registry"]

[tool.mypy]
strict = true
exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
pythonpath = ["src"]
testpaths = ["tests"]

   127→3. **test_registry.py engine fixture uses file-based engine:** The `engine` fixture in test_registry.py uses `tmp_path` (file-based) rather than `:memory:`, which means WAL mode is active for all CRUD tests as well — a stricter test environment than required.
   128→
   129→---

   132→
   133→No gaps. All 14 must-have truths are verified by code inspection and passing automated tests. All 5 AREG requirements are satisfied. All 3 key links are wired correctly. No anti-patterns detected. Static analysis is clean.

requires-python = ">=3.12"

[tool.ruff]

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]

asyncio_mode = "auto"

[tool.coverage.run]

]
omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/.venv/*", "*/venv/*"]

[tool.coverage.report]

    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
]

input.content:
# Phase 24: Phase 22 Verification & Dependency Hygiene - Research

**Researched:** 2026-03-06
**Domain:** Monorepo dependency declarations, GSD VERIFICATION.md pattern, SUMMARY.md frontmatter schema
**Confidence:** HIGH

---

<phase_requirements>
## Phase Requirements

| ID | Description | Research Support |
|----|-------------|-----------------|
| DTOL-01 | DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture | Implementation confirmed in hypernetwork.py; 7/7 tests pass; requires VERIFICATION.md to formally close |
| DTOL-02 | Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s) | Confirmed via test_hypernetwork.py; safetensors undeclared in pyproject.toml |
| DTOL-03 | Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format) | save_hypernetwork_adapter() confirmed; safetensors undeclared in pyproject.toml |
| EVAL-01 | User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset() | 21/21 eval tests pass; function implemented; zero production callers (acceptable — lib-level) |
| EVAL-02 | User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k() | Unbiased Chen 2021 estimator implemented and tested |
| EVAL-03 | Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold) | run_kill_switch_gate() implemented; disconnection from training pipeline is accepted tech debt (per audit recommendation) |
</phase_requirements>

---

## Summary

Phase 24 is a documentation and dependency hygiene phase with no new feature code. All six requirements (DTOL-01/02/03, EVAL-01/02/03) have working implementations from Phase 22 that pass tests. The closure gap is purely administrative: Phase 22 never produced a VERIFICATION.md, one SUMMARY.md uses the wrong frontmatter field name, and two runtime package dependencies are undeclared in `model-training/pyproject.toml`.

The audit (v5.0-MILESTONE-AUDIT.md) explicitly states the code is implemented and tests pass — the only missing artifact is formal verification. The plan for this phase is therefore: (1) fix `model-training/pyproject.toml` to declare `adapter-registry` and `safetensors`, (2) fix `22-01-SUMMARY.md` to use `requirements-completed` instead of `requirements`, and (3) write `22-VERIFICATION.md` documenting that all six requirements are satisfied. The full test suite currently shows 151 passed, 4 skipped, 12 xfailed — no regressions are expected from the dependency declaration changes.

**Primary recommendation:** One plan, three tasks in sequence: pyproject.toml fix → SUMMARY frontmatter fix → VERIFICATION.md creation. All three are low-risk edits with no code changes.

---

## Standard Stack

### Core
| Tool | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
|------|---------|---------|--------------|
| uv workspace | Current | Monorepo package management | Project standard — all libs use `[tool.uv.sources]` for workspace deps |
| hatchling | Current | Build backend for all libs | Project standard — all pyproject.toml files use hatchling |
| safetensors | >=0.4.0 | Tensor serialization for adapter files | Already in use transitively via peft; needs explicit declaration |
| adapter-registry | workspace | LoRA adapter metadata storage | Already imported at runtime in trainer.py lines 174-175 |

### No New Libraries Required

This phase adds no new code. It only:
1. Adds two missing dependency declarations to an existing pyproject.toml
2. Renames one YAML frontmatter key in an existing SUMMARY.md
3. Creates a new VERIFICATION.md (documentation only)

---

## Architecture Patterns

### Workspace Dependency Declaration Pattern

In this monorepo, workspace packages are declared in TWO places:

**1. `[project] dependencies` list** — the package name (hyphen-separated):
```toml
dependencies = [
    "shared",
    "adapter-registry",   # <-- add this
    "safetensors>=0.4.0", # <-- add this
    "torch>=2.0.0",
    ...
]
```

**2. `[tool.uv.sources]` section** — maps package name to workspace source:
```toml
[tool.uv.sources]
shared = { workspace = true }
adapter-registry = { workspace = true }  # <-- add this
```

Third-party packages (like `safetensors`) do NOT need a `[tool.uv.sources]` entry — they are resolved from PyPI.

**Reference:** `libs/model-training/pyproject.toml` already has this pattern for `shared`. The `libs/training-svc` service (which already correctly declares `adapter-registry`) serves as a cross-check model.

### SUMMARY.md Frontmatter Schema

The GSD tooling (`gsd-tools.cjs summary-extract`) reads the `requirements-completed` key to track which requirements a plan closes. The `requirements` key is not recognized.

**Incorrect (current 22-01-SUMMARY.md line 28):**
```yaml
requirements: [DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03]
```

**Correct:**
```yaml
requirements-completed: [DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03]
```

**Reference:** `22-02-SUMMARY.md` line 49 uses the correct key (`requirements-completed: [EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03]`). `22-03-SUMMARY.md` line 36 also uses the correct key. Only `22-01-SUMMARY.md` uses the wrong key.

### VERIFICATION.md Structure

The GSD verifier pattern is established by Phase 18 and Phase 23 examples. Every VERIFICATION.md has:

1. YAML frontmatter: `phase`, `verified`, `status`, `score`, `re_verification`
2. `## Goal Achievement` section with Observable Truths table
3. Required Artifacts table (file path → expected → status → details)
4. Key Link Verification table (from → to → via → status)
5. Requirements Coverage table (req ID → source plan → description → status → evidence)
6. Anti-Patterns Found table
7. Human Verification Required section
8. Static Analysis Results table (mypy/ruff/pytest)
9. Commit Verification table

**Phase 22 verification scope:**
- DTOL-01: `DocToLoraHypernetwork` class exists in `libs/model-training/src/model_training/hypernetwork.py` with Perceiver architecture
- DTOL-02: `forward()` returns rank-8 LoRA state_dict in single forward pass; `test_hypernetwork_forward_peft_keys` and `test_hypernetwork_forward_lora_shapes` tests (skipped on CPU with xfail, confirming GPU behavior documented)
- DTOL-03: `save_hypernetwork_adapter()` writes `adapter_model.safetensors` + `adapter_config.json` in PEFT format; `test_save_hypernetwork_adapter_writes_files` + `test_save_hypernetwork_adapter_config_fields` PASS
- EVAL-01: `run_humaneval_subset()` implemented with bundled 20-task JSON; 8 tests pass
- EVAL-02: `calculate_pass_at_k()` unbiased estimator (Chen 2021); 3 tests pass
- EVAL-03: `run_kill_switch_gate()` 5% threshold verdict; 4 tests pass

**Note on EVAL-03 and kill-switch disconnection:** The audit flags "kill-switch gate disconnected from training pipeline" as tech debt, not as a requirement failure. EVAL-03 as specified in REQUIREMENTS.md says "Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)" — this is a *library function capability*, not a requirement that it be wired into every training path. The VERIFICATION.md should note the tech debt while confirming EVAL-03 is satisfied at the library level.

---

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
| Dependency version constraints | Manual safetensors version research | Match existing peft version floor (>=0.4.0 is safe; peft already requires it) | Transitive dep version already validated by existing tests passing |
| VERIFICATION.md template | Custom structure | Copy Phase 23's VERIFICATION.md structure exactly | GSD tooling and reviewer expectations follow established pattern |

---

## Common Pitfalls

### Pitfall 1: Adding safetensors to `[tool.uv.sources]`
**What goes wrong:** Trying to add `safetensors = { workspace = true }` alongside the dependency declaration
**Why it happens:** Confusing PyPI packages with workspace packages
**How to avoid:** Only workspace packages (from `[tool.uv.workspace]` members) go in `[tool.uv.sources]`. `safetensors` is a PyPI package — it only needs a `dependencies` entry.
**Warning signs:** uv error "package not found in workspace members"

### Pitfall 2: Forgetting the `[tool.uv.sources]` entry for adapter-registry
**What goes wrong:** Adding `adapter-registry` to `dependencies` list but omitting `adapter-registry = { workspace = true }` in `[tool.uv.sources]`
**Why it happens:** Two-step process is easy to miss the second step
**How to avoid:** Check existing `shared` entry as the pattern — it appears in both `dependencies` and `[tool.uv.sources]`
**Warning signs:** uv install error "adapter-registry not found on PyPI"

### Pitfall 3: Editing 22-02-SUMMARY.md instead of 22-01-SUMMARY.md
**What goes wrong:** Fixing the wrong file — 22-02 already uses `requirements-completed` correctly
**Why it happens:** Two SUMMARY files in the same directory
**How to avoid:** The audit explicitly states "22-01-SUMMARY uses `requirements` field instead of `requirements-completed`". Only 22-01 needs the fix.

### Pitfall 4: VERIFICATION.md claims full suite at wrong count
**What goes wrong:** Writing "151 tests pass" when Phase 22 tests are a subset
**How to avoid:** The VERIFICATION.md should cite specific test files and counts, then reference the full suite result separately for the zero-regressions check.

### Pitfall 5: Treating EVAL-03 as unverified due to pipeline disconnection
**What goes wrong:** Marking EVAL-03 as PARTIAL because kill-switch is not called by training jobs
**Why it happens:** Conflating integration gap (tech debt) with library capability (requirement)
**How to avoid:** EVAL-03 requires the gate function exists and works — it does. The disconnection from `_run_training_job` is tracked as tech debt in the audit, not as an EVAL-03 requirement gap.

---

## Code Examples

### Adding adapter-registry as workspace dependency

**File:** `libs/model-training/pyproject.toml`

```toml
# In [project] dependencies:
dependencies = [
    "shared",
    "adapter-registry",
    "safetensors>=0.4.0",
    "torch>=2.0.0",
    "peft>=0.18.0",
    "bitsandbytes>=0.45.0",
    "transformers>=4.47.0",
    "trl>=0.16.0",
    "datasets>=3.0.0",
]

# In [tool.uv.sources]:
[tool.uv.sources]
shared = { workspace = true }
adapter-registry = { workspace = true }
```

### Fixing 22-01-SUMMARY.md frontmatter

**File:** `.planning/phases/22-kill-switch-gate/22-01-SUMMARY.md`

Change line 28 from:
```yaml
requirements: [DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03]
```
To:
```yaml
requirements-completed: [DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03]
```

### VERIFICATION.md frontmatter for Phase 22

```yaml
---
phase: 22-kill-switch-gate
verified: 2026-03-06T00:00:00Z
status: passed
score: 6/6 requirements verified
re_verification: false
---
```

---

## State of the Art

| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
| `requirements:` in SUMMARY.md | `requirements-completed:` | Established by Phase 19+ SUMMARYs | gsd-tools summary-extract returns correct list |
| Transitive-only dep (safetensors via peft) | Explicit dep declaration | Phase 24 fix | model-training independently installable |
| No VERIFICATION.md | VERIFICATION.md with Observable Truths table | Phase 18 pattern | Formal closure of requirements |

---

## Open Questions

1. **safetensors version floor**
   - What we know: peft>=0.18.0 requires safetensors>=0.4.3 transitively
   - What's unclear: Whether to specify `>=0.4.0` or `>=0.4.3` or no version pin
   - Recommendation: Use `safetensors>=0.4.0` — conservative floor that won't conflict with peft's own constraint; peft will enforce the stricter version if needed

2. **DTOL-02 forward pass timing (<1s) test evidence**
   - What we know: `test_hypernetwork_forward_peft_keys` and `test_hypernetwork_forward_lora_shapes` are marked `xfail` with `pytest.importorskip("torch")` — they skip on CPU but document expected GPU behavior
   - What's unclear: Whether the VERIFICATION.md should note this as GPU-verified or CPU-verified
   - Recommendation: Note that forward-pass tests use small model (hidden_dim=32, num_layers=1-2) on CPU CI to confirm architecture correctness; <1s timing is a design property verified by architecture analysis (single forward pass, no gradient descent), not by timed test

---

## Validation Architecture

### Test Framework
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Framework | pytest + pytest-xdist |
| Config file | root pyproject.toml `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` |
| Quick run command | `uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/ libs/evaluation/tests/ -x -v` |
| Full suite command | `uv run pytest -x` |

### Phase Requirements to Test Map
| Req ID | Behavior | Test Type | Automated Command | File Exists? |

    69→Recent decisions affecting v5.0:
    70→
    71→- vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image used; openai removed from pip install (bundled in vLLM image); VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True enables runtime adapter loading
    72→- lora-server host port changed from 8000 to 8100 in docker-compose; container-internal port stays 8000 (startup.sh unchanged)
    73→- rune_data:/data shared volume added to api and lora-server in docker-compose for cross-service SQLite access
    74→- Hardware validation deferred — user will validate hardware separately; not a roadmap phase
    75→- Provider abstraction added — InferenceProvider interface (INF-01) with VLLMProvider (INF-02), OllamaProvider (INF-03), factory (INF-04), per-step model/provider config (INF-07)
    76→- AGENT-01 is backend-agnostic — calls InferenceProvider.generate(), not VLLMClient directly
    77→- Build order: adapter-registry → inference abstraction + providers → agent loop + trajectory → QLoRA training → kill-switch gate
    78→- INFRA-01/02/03 in Phase 19 (lora-server config + port fix); INFRA-04/05 in Phase 21 (GPU deps)
    79→- evolution-svc explicitly out of scope for v5.0 (deferred to v6+)
    80→- bfloat16 hardcoded in build_qlora_config (float16 causes silent NaN loss at 7B scale)
    81→- max_loras=2, max_lora_rank=64, gpu_memory_utilization=0.80 to avoid VRAM OOM at startup
    82→- Engine from sqlalchemy.engine not sqlmodel — SQLModel does not re-export Engine (discovered 18-01, confirmed 18-02)
    83→- Session-per-method pattern with expire_on_commit=False + expunge() — required to prevent DetachedInstanceError when returning records from closed sessions (established 18-01)
    84→- WAL test must use file-based engine (tmp_path), not :memory: — WAL has no practical effect on in-memory SQLite
    85→- memory_engine fixture: create_engine("sqlite:///:memory:") with function scope gives per-test isolation at zero cleanup cost
    86→- [Phase 19-01]: asyncio_mode=auto added to inference lib pyproject.toml — lib-scoped pytest overrides root config
    87→- [Phase 19-01]: VLLMProvider.generate() passes adapter_id as model param — vLLM LoRA routing mechanism requires lora_name in the model field
    88→- [Phase 19-01]: VLLM_BASE_URL default set to port 8100 (api-service owns port 8000)
    89→- [Phase 19-03]: os.environ.get() used in factory.py instead of os.getenv() — mypy cannot narrow os.getenv(key, str_default) return type to str; explicit variable annotations resolve the issue
    90→- [Phase 19-03]: Env var reads placed inside get_provider() function body (not module level) — allows monkeypatch.setenv() to work in tests; module-level reads captured at import time
    91→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR read inside function body for monkeypatch testability (same pattern as Phase 19 factory.py)
    92→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: record_trajectory() extended with keyword-only task_description/task_type/adapter_ids args for save_trajectory_node compatibility
    93→- [Phase 20-agent-loop]: format_for_sft() uses reversed(steps) to find last tests_passed=True step as assistant content
    94→- [Phase 20-02]: RUNE_MODEL and RUNE_EXEC_TIMEOUT env vars read inside function bodies for monkeypatch testability — same pattern as Phase 19 factory.py
    95→- [Phase 20-02]: py.typed markers added to inference and model-training libs — correct PEP 561 solution for mypy strict import-untyped compliance
    96→- [Phase 20-02]: reflect_node uses list concatenation (state["trajectory"] + [step]) not .append() — LangGraph requires immutable state updates
    97→- [Phase 21-01]: bfloat16 compute dtype set in BitsAndBytesConfig, NOT LoraConfig — LoraConfig is purely about LoRA layer structure
    98→- [Phase 21-01]: No modules_to_save in LoraConfig — would break vLLM adapter loading (include embed_tokens/lm_head in saved PEFT artifact)
    99→- [Phase 21-01]: sys.modules injection pattern for mocking deferred GPU imports in CPU CI — unittest.mock.patch("peft.X") fails when peft is not installed
   100→- [Phase 21-01]: RUNE_ADAPTER_DIR/RUNE_BASE_MODEL/RUNE_DATABASE_URL env vars read inside function bodies for monkeypatch testability
   101→- [Phase 21-02]: Deferred import of model_training.trainer inside _run_training_job body — prevents GPU lib import at startup in CPU-only environments (same INFRA-05 pattern)
   102→- [Phase 21-02]: JOB_STORE as module-level dict — state is lost on restart, acceptable for single-user local MVP
   103→- [Phase 21-02]: _run_training_job is a regular (non-async) function — FastAPI BackgroundTasks runs it in thread pool executor
   104→- [Phase 21-02]: Mock pattern: patch training_svc.routers.training._run_training_job in tests to prevent model_training.trainer GPU import
   105→- [Phase 22-01]: DocToLoraHypernetwork uses _LazyHypernetworkProxy — real nn.Module subclass built on first instantiation to keep module importable without torch (INFRA-05)
   106→- [Phase 22-01]: save_hypernetwork_adapter: modules_to_save=None — vLLM rejects embed_tokens/lm_head in adapter artifacts (consistent with Phase 21-01 decision)
   107→- [Phase 22-01]: Torch tests use hidden_dim=32, num_layers=1-2 to keep CPU CI fast — large linear weight_head (8192 x 3.67M) hangs indefinitely on CPU with default 7B params
   108→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: run_humaneval_subset accepts Optional completions dict — None raises NotImplementedError; inference wiring deferred to orchestration level
   109→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: data/ directory un-ignored via local .gitignore in evaluation/src/evaluation/ — root .gitignore has global data/ exclusion
   110→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: sys.executable used in subprocess (not hardcoded python) — matches current venv Python in all execution environments
   111→- [Phase 22-kill-switch-gate]: _run_hypernetwork_job uses first trajectory_id from request.trajectory_ids — single trajectory drives the hypernetwork forward pass
   112→- [Phase 23-01]: Use training_svc.storage.engine (not RUNE_DATABASE_URL) in _run_hypernetwork_job — service-level function shares service DB, ensures QLoRA and hypernetwork adapters in same database
   113→- [Phase 23-01]: model_training added to workspace-packages mypy override block (not GPU libs block) — it is a local workspace package, not a GPU-only library
   114→- [Phase 23-01]: All new imports deferred inside _run_hypernetwork_job function body per INFRA-05 — allows patch("training_svc.storage.engine", ...) in tests

    67→## Phase Details
    68→
    69→### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
    70→**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
    71→**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
    72→**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
    73→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    74→  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
    75→  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
    76→  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
    77→  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
    78→  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
    79→**Plans**: 2 plans
    80→
    81→Plans:
    82→- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
    83→- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05
    84→
    85→### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
    86→**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
    87→**Depends on**: Phase 18
    88→**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
    89→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    90→  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
    91→  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
    92→  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
    93→  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
    94→  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
    95→  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
    96→**Plans**: 3 plans
    97→
    98→Plans:
    99→- [x] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage — DONE 2026-03-05
   100→- [x] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix — DONE 2026-03-05
   101→- [x] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs — DONE 2026-03-05

   103→### Phase 20: Agent Loop
   104→**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
   105→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
   106→**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
   107→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   108→  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
   109→  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
   110→  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
   111→  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
   112→  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
   113→**Plans**: 2 plans
   114→
   115→Plans:
   116→- [x] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep — DONE 2026-03-05
   117→- [x] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites — DONE 2026-03-05
   118→
   119→### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
   120→**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
   121→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
   122→**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
   123→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   124→  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
   125→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   126→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   127→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
   128→  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
   129→**Plans**: 2 plans

   133→- [x] 21-02-PLAN.md — Wire training-svc endpoints: POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id} with background job dispatch — DONE 2026-03-05

   135→### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
   136→**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
   137→**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
   138→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
   139→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   140→  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
   141→  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
   142→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   143→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   144→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
   145→**Plans**: 3 plans

1→# Requirements: Rune
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v5.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for first working implementation. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Adapter Registry
    11→
    12→- [x] **AREG-01**: User can store a new adapter record with metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source) via AdapterRegistry.store()
    13→- [x] **AREG-02**: User can retrieve an adapter record by ID via AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id()
    14→- [x] **AREG-03**: User can query adapters by task_type via AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type()
    15→- [x] **AREG-04**: User can list all non-archived adapters via AdapterRegistry.list_all()
    16→- [x] **AREG-05**: AdapterRegistry uses SQLite with WAL mode and engine-parameterized constructor for shared database access
    17→
    18→### Inference
    19→
    20→- [x] **INF-01**: Abstract InferenceProvider interface with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), list_adapters() methods
    21→- [x] **INF-02**: VLLMProvider implementation with full LoRA hot-loading support (POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, /v1/unload_lora_adapter)
    22→- [x] **INF-03**: OllamaProvider implementation for inference via Ollama API (LoRA support where Ollama supports it)
    23→- [x] **INF-04**: Provider factory/registry for selecting backend by configuration (env var or config file)
    24→- [x] **INF-05**: User can generate with a specific loaded adapter by passing adapter name as model parameter
    25→- [x] **INF-06**: User can load multiple adapters simultaneously for composition (provider-dependent, graceful degradation)
    26→- [x] **INF-07**: Per-step model/provider configuration — agent can use different models or providers for different steps (e.g., one model for generation, another for reflection)
    27→
    28→### Agent Loop
    29→
    30→- [x] **AGENT-01**: generate_node calls InferenceProvider.generate() with task description and optional adapter, returning generated code (backend-agnostic)
    31→- [x] **AGENT-02**: execute_node runs generated code in a sandboxed subprocess with timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exit_code/tests_passed
    32→- [x] **AGENT-03**: reflect_node accumulates trajectory data (attempt count, code, results) without LLM call
    33→- [x] **AGENT-04**: save_trajectory_node persists trajectory via record_trajectory() and sets outcome
    34→- [x] **AGENT-05**: RuneState includes session_id field for trajectory persistence
    35→- [x] **AGENT-06**: Agent loop closes end-to-end: generate → execute → reflect → retry/save with should_retry routing
    36→
    37→### Training Pipeline
    38→
    39→- [x] **TRAIN-01**: User can record a coding trajectory as structured JSON via record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)
    40→- [x] **TRAIN-02**: User can convert trajectory data to SFT chat format via format_for_sft()
    41→- [x] **TRAIN-03**: User can create a QLoRA config with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype via build_qlora_config()
    42→- [x] **TRAIN-04**: User can apply a LoRA adapter to a base model via apply_lora_adapter()
    43→- [x] **TRAIN-05**: QLoRA training pipeline runs end-to-end: trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → store in registry
    44→- [x] **TRAIN-06**: training-svc exposes POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking
    45→- [x] **TRAIN-07**: training-svc pyproject.toml declares model-training as workspace dependency
    46→
    47→### Doc-to-LoRA Hypernetwork
    48→
    49→- [x] **DTOL-01**: DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture
    50→- [x] **DTOL-02**: Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s)
    51→- [x] **DTOL-03**: Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format)
    52→- [x] **DTOL-04**: training-svc exposes POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint for Doc-to-LoRA training
    53→
    54→### Evaluation & Kill-Switch
    55→
    56→- [x] **EVAL-01**: User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset()
    57→- [x] **EVAL-02**: User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k()
    58→- [x] **EVAL-03**: Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)
    59→
    60→### Infrastructure
    61→
    62→- [x] **INFRA-01**: lora-server Dockerfile uses vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image (not python:3.12-slim)
    63→- [x] **INFRA-02**: lora-server sets VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True environment variable
    64→- [x] **INFRA-03**: docker-compose resolves port conflict (api-service and lora-server on different host ports)
    65→- [x] **INFRA-04**: model-training pyproject.toml adds GPU dependencies (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) with TYPE_CHECKING guards
    66→- [x] **INFRA-05**: All GPU imports deferred inside function bodies (not top-level) for CPU-only CI compatibility
    67→
    68→## Future Requirements
    69→
    70→Deferred to v6+. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    71→
    72→### Evolution
    73→
    74→- **EVOL-01**: Evolution operator scores adapter fitness based on HumanEval pass rate
    75→- **EVOL-02**: Evolution operator performs adapter crossover via weight interpolation
    76→- **EVOL-03**: Evolution operator archives low-fitness adapters
    77→
    78→### Advanced Features
    79→
    80→- **ADV-01**: Embedding-based automatic adapter selection via vector similarity search
    81→- **ADV-02**: HITL UI with session monitoring, adapter library browser, trajectory viewer
    82→- **ADV-03**: Docker container isolation for code execution (replace subprocess sandbox)
    83→
    84→## Out of Scope
    85→
    86→Explicitly excluded. Documented to prevent scope creep.
    87→
    88→| Feature | Reason |
    89→|---------|--------|
    90→| Hardware validation phase | User deferred — will validate hardware separately |
    91→| Tensor parallelism (TP=2) | vLLM bug #21471 — corrupted output on PCIe GPUs without NVLink |
    92→| Adapter merging into base weights | Loses hot-swap composability; vLLM Punica kernels handle LoRA with near-zero overhead |
    93→| Cloud API inference | Hard constraint: local-first, no cloud dependencies for inference. Provider abstraction supports local backends (vLLM, Ollama) only |
    94→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM — must schedule sequentially |
    95→| Streaming responses in agent loop | Agent needs complete code, not token stream; add to API layer separately if needed |
    96→| Multi-tenant isolation | Single-user local system; session_id provides sufficient organization |
    97→| evolution-svc implementation | Depends on populated adapter library from multiple sessions; v6+ |
